approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the intragroup monetary receivable/payable
approaches.rationale: A recognised intragroup monetary item may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements for foreign currency risk if the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a monetary item
Its foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
Only the foreign currency risk component is designated if that component is separately identifiable and reliably measurable
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met at inception

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign-currency cash flows
approaches.rationale: A cash flow hedge may be used for foreign currency risk on a recognised asset or liability, or on a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction, if that risk could affect consolidated profit or loss.
conditions: If applied to a recognised intragroup item, the foreign exchange effects are not fully eliminated on consolidation
If applied before recognition, the intragroup transaction is highly probable
A forecast intragroup transaction is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
The foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met at inception

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: This hedge model applies only to the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation and not by analogy to an intragroup dividend receivable.
conditions: It would become relevant only if the hedged item were an amount of net assets of a foreign operation rather than the dividend receivable
